overall_assessment: The corrected RR-AUTH-04 packet is materially stronger than the earlier baseline, but it is not critique-clean because the shipped public-auth risk-matrix still does not pin real subject-level throttle ceilings for the live password/login-reset domains. The bounded evidence only proves cross-IP subject throttling after test-local override injection, so the packet still carries a distributed abuse-control gap on an explicitly in-scope public auth surface.

findings.title: Live public auth domains still ship without enforced subject-rate ceilings
findings.rationale: The real RR-AUTH-04 entries in `laravel-app/config/api_security.php` define `subject_input` and `subject_kind`, but they do not define `subject_requests_per_minute` for `tenant_public_password_login`, `tenant_public_password_reset_token`, `tenant_public_password_reset`, `landlord_public_password_login`, `landlord_public_password_reset_token`, or `landlord_public_password_reset`. The only packet evidence that proves cross-IP subject throttling appears in test-specific override setup where those same domains are mutated to inject `subject_requests_per_minute => 1` before the assertion runs, and the architecture guardrail only regex-checks for `subject_input` plus `fail_closed_on_backend_error` rather than requiring a real subject ceiling. That leaves the corrected baseline materially exposed to distributed credential-stuffing or reset-spam attempts spread across many IPs even though the review reconciliation ledger currently records the cross-IP subject-identity concern as resolved.
findings.suggested_action: Add explicit `subject_requests_per_minute` ceilings for the shipped tenant and landlord public login/reset domains, extend the guardrail to fail when those fields are missing, and keep RR-AUTH-04 blocked until the live config and non-override regression proofs show the distributed subject bucket is actually enforced on the release baseline.
